headline: March jobs report, Fed, Nike earnings: What to Watch Next Week
summary: Asking for a Trend Host Josh Lipton previews several of the biggest stories to come throughout next week, including the March jobs report due out next Friday, April 3; commentary from top Federal Reserve officials, including chair Jerome Powell; and earnings results from a slew of companies such as Nike (NKE), Lamb Weston (LW), and Tilray Brands (TLRY).

headline: Jobs Report, Nike Earnings, Gas Prices: What to Watch Next Week

Auto executives will also gather in New York for a conference where higher gasoline prices will be top-of-mind.

headline: Nike’s World Cup Textile Waste Line Tests Turnaround And Valuation Story
summary: Nike, NYSE:NKE, has launched 2026 World Cup fan and performance apparel made entirely from textile waste. The new line is part of the company’s ongoing turnaround plan and is aimed at both fans and professional athletes. The collection is designed to spotlight circular materials while tying Nike’s brand closely to one of the biggest global sporting events. Nike is rolling out this World Cup collection at a time when its shares trade around $52.07 and longer term returns have been weak, with...
